fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let B be a category. Define the displayed category \overline { B } over B as follows:

fr:ol.fr:li: For x \in B, define \overline { B } _{ x } as the collection of pairs ( \bar {x} \in B,p _{ x } :x \to \bar {x}).
For f : x \to y \in B, define \overline { B } _{ f } to be the collection of commuting squares in the following configuration:

fr:embedded-tex.fr:embedded-tex-body: \DiagramSquare { height = 1.7cm, sw = \bar {x}, se = \bar {y}, nw = x, ne = y, west = p _{ x } , east = p _{ y } , south = \bar {f}, north = f, west/style = {->,exists}, east/style = {->,exists}, south/style = {->,exists}, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Dually to the fundamental self-indexing, every category B can also be displayed over itself via its coslices B ^{ \setminus x }.
Let B be a category. Define the displayed category \overline { B } over B as follows:

fr:ol.fr:li: For x \in B, define \overline { B } _{ x } as the collection of pairs ( \bar {x} \in B,p _{ x } :x \to \bar {x}).
For f : x \to y \in B, define \overline { B } _{ f } to be the collection of commuting squares in the following configuration:

fr:embedded-tex.fr:embedded-tex-body: \DiagramSquare { height = 1.7cm, sw = \bar {x}, se = \bar {y}, nw = x, ne = y, west = p _{ x } , east = p _{ y } , south = \bar {f}, north = f, west/style = {->,exists}, east/style = {->,exists}, south/style = {->,exists}, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Prove that \overline { B } is a cocartesian fibration if and only if B has pushouts.
Prove that the total category of \overline { B } is the arrow category B ^{ \to }, and the projection is the domain functor.
Prove that \overline { B } is a cartesian fibration for any category B.
